summary: This is a detailed Islamic lecture from the Muslim Community Center of Tucson covering the concept of Iman (faith) in Islam and its distinction from mere belief. The speaker explains that Iman is not simply intellectual acceptance but a comprehensive state involving the heart, tongue, and actions. The lecture uses Quranic verses to establish that true Iman drives believers to implement their faith through worship and righteous deeds, and that Shirk (associating partners with Allah) corrupts Iman just as ritual impurity nullifies ablution. The speaker then transitions into discussing the story of Prophet Musa and the Bani Israel, particularly the incident at the Red Sea and the subsequent test of their faith when they encountered idol-worshipping peoples.
suggested_title: Understanding Iman (Faith) in Islam and the Story of Prophet Musa at the Red Sea
catchy_title: True Faith: More Than Just Belief
